content.full_text:
November 2, 1982

Marcy DeJesus
Mothers Against Drunk Driving (MADD)
P.O. Box L-164
Tarzana, CA 91356

Re: Mailings
Our Letter No. A-82-184

Dear Ms. DeJesus:

Thank you for your telephone call of this date relating that a candidate for the California Assembly had made a mass mailing (200 copies or more of nearly identical pieces of mail) consisting of an unauthorized copy of a MADD letter to his opponent. His opponent then engaged in somewhat similar conduct. You have requested advice as to what aspects of the Political Reform Act ("Act")* apply to your situation.

I have advised you that the Act requires the "sender" of a "mass mailing" to be identified on the outside of the envelope and at least one of the inserts. (Section 84305.) Our current regulation (2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18435) defines the "sender" as the one who pays for the mailing. As you describe the facts, this was not MADD.

We are in the process of proposing amendments to Regulation 18435, which will be heard by the Commission on December 6, 1982. I enclose a copy of the Notice and the Proposed Changes. We would invite MADD to comment on these. We would also urge you to forward to us copies of these mailings for our rule-making file.

*Government Code Sections 81000-91014 (copy enclosed). All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise specified.

Should you wish to make a complaint regarding these matters, you may contact this office or our Los Angeles office. In Sacramento, the number is (916) 322-6441, in Los Angeles, the number is (213) 720-5196 and the address there is 107 South Broadway, Suite 7007, 90012.

Should you have further questions of me, I may be reached at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

[Signature]
Robert E. Leidigh
Counsel
Legal Division

REL:plh
Enclosures
